title: AI-901 Mock Exam 2: Applied AI Solutions
description: A 50-question unofficial mock paper aligned to the AI-901 skills measured from April 15, 2026. Uses realistic dropdown, statement, single-choice, and multiple-answer formats with original questions and official Microsoft Learn references.

domains.title: Implement AI solutions by using Microsoft Foundry

items.prompt: A public benefits assistant excludes keyboard-only users from completing an application. Which responsible-AI principle is most directly affected?
items.explanation: Inclusiveness requires experiences that work for people with varied abilities and interaction needs. Grounding, elasticity, and image creation do not address the accessibility barrier.

items.prompt: A model performs well during testing but drifts after customer behavior changes. Which practice best supports reliability and safety?
items.answer: Monitor production quality and define thresholds for intervention
options: Monitor production quality and define thresholds for intervention
Assume the original test score remains valid
Increase output length for every request
items.explanation: Reliability is a lifecycle concern, so teams monitor real behavior and respond when quality changes. A historic score cannot guarantee future performance, and output length is unrelated.

items.prompt: {{blank}} is supported by documenting who owns an AI system and approves high-impact changes.
items.explanation: Accountability assigns responsibility and governance for system decisions and changes. The alternative terms describe technical workloads or infrastructure behavior.

items.prompt: Decide whether each action supports transparent use of AI.

statements.text: Tell users when they are interacting with an automated system
Hide known limitations because users might lose confidence
Explain the main factors used for a consequential recommendation

items.explanation: Transparency gives people useful notice and understandable information about operation and limitations. Concealing known limitations prevents informed use rather than improving it.

items.prompt: Which TWO evaluation choices best support fairness?
answers: Compare error rates across relevant user groups
Use representative data that includes affected populations
options: Compare error rates across relevant user groups
Use representative data that includes affected populations
Exclude difficult cases from every test set
items.explanation: Group-level metrics and representative data can expose unequal performance hidden by averages. Removing difficult cases and reporting only aggregates can conceal harms.

items.prompt: Which TWO controls are most appropriate for a high-impact automated decision?
answers: Provide meaningful human review with authority to change the outcome
Give affected people a clear appeal or correction path
options: Provide meaningful human review with authority to change the outcome
Give affected people a clear appeal or correction path
Treat the model score as automatically final
Remove records of how the decision was reached
items.explanation: Human oversight and recourse keep consequential decisions accountable and correctable. Automatic finality and missing records reduce the ability to identify or remedy errors.

items.prompt: What is the primary purpose of an embedding model?
items.answer: Represent content as numeric vectors for similarity tasks
options: Represent content as numeric vectors for similarity tasks
items.explanation: Embeddings encode semantic characteristics as vectors that can support search and comparison. They do not synthesize speech, manage authorization, or create infrastructure.

items.prompt: A model invents facts when it lacks current product information. Which change most directly addresses the missing knowledge?
items.answer: Retrieve approved current content and include it as grounding context
options: Retrieve approved current content and include it as grounding context
items.explanation: Grounding supplies relevant current evidence at inference time. Sampling variation, identity text, and output modality do not add trustworthy product knowledge.

items.prompt: A team is comparing two model deployments for a mobile assistant. Which test set is most useful?
items.answer: Representative mobile requests including expected edge cases
options: Representative mobile requests including expected edge cases
Only the shortest successful demo prompt
Unrelated benchmark questions from another domain
One prompt selected by the model vendor
items.explanation: Evaluation should reflect real requests, constraints, and failure cases for the intended application. Narrow demos or unrelated benchmarks do not establish production suitability.

items.prompt: Increasing {{blank}} generally allows more variation in generated wording.
items.explanation: Temperature influences the randomness of token sampling and therefore output variation. Citations, authorization scope, and storage do not serve as sampling controls.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about model selection.

statements.text: Latency and cost can matter even when two models have similar quality
The largest model is always the correct production choice
Regional availability never constrains a deployment decision

items.explanation: A deployment choice balances measured quality with latency, cost, and regional availability. Neither model size nor an assumption of universal availability determines the best fit.

items.prompt: Which TWO steps help evaluate a grounded assistant?
answers: Measure whether answers are supported by retrieved sources
Test retrieval with representative user wording
options: Measure whether answers are supported by retrieved sources
Test retrieval with representative user wording
Judge only the fluency of one answer
items.explanation: Grounded systems require both relevant retrieval and source-supported generation. Fluency alone can hide unsupported claims, and disabling retrieval removes the behavior under test.

items.prompt: A service predicts whether a transaction is fraudulent or legitimate. Which task is this?
items.explanation: Binary classification chooses between two labels such as fraud and legitimate. Regression returns a number, clustering discovers groups, and summarization condenses text.

items.prompt: A retailer groups customers by similar behavior without predefined segment labels. Which technique should it use?
items.explanation: Clustering discovers natural groups in unlabeled data. Classification requires known labels, while speech synthesis and OCR process different modalities.

items.prompt: Which capability identifies the main topics in a document as short phrases?
items.explanation: Key phrase extraction returns salient words or phrases from text. Detection, speech recognition, and regression solve visual, audio, and numeric tasks.

items.prompt: A system must identify each pixel that belongs to a road in a satellite image. Which vision task is required?
items.explanation: Semantic segmentation assigns a class to image pixels and can outline regions such as roads. Whole-image labels and language techniques do not provide pixel-level output.

items.prompt: A legal team needs a short faithful synopsis of each contract while retaining the original documents. Which workload fits?
items.explanation: Summarization condenses source text into a shorter account while the original remains the evidence. The other workloads create media or detect unusual numeric patterns.

items.prompt: A service must produce a natural-language answer that did not previously exist. Which workload category applies?
items.explanation: Generative AI creates new content such as a response. OCR extracts existing characters, while clustering and anomaly detection analyze patterns in data.

items.prompt: {{blank}} is the image task that predicts which known plant species appears in a photograph.
items.explanation: Image classification assigns a known label to a whole image. Regression predicts a number, translation changes language, and synthesis creates audio.

items.prompt: Decide whether each capability matches the requested output.

statements.text: OCR can return printed characters from an image
Sentiment analysis returns bounding boxes around products
Speech synthesis can create audio from text

items.explanation: OCR extracts visible text and speech synthesis creates spoken output. Bounding boxes are associated with object detection, not sentiment analysis.

items.prompt: Which TWO characteristics distinguish an agentic solution from a simple text generator?
answers: It can choose among configured tools
It can take sequenced actions toward a goal
options: It can choose among configured tools
It can take sequenced actions toward a goal
It must always generate an image
It requires every step to be manually selected by the user
items.explanation: Agentic solutions can reason about available actions and use tools toward a goal. Image generation is optional, and fully manual step selection removes the defining autonomy.

items.prompt: Which TWO techniques analyze written customer comments without generating new text?
items.explanation: Sentiment and entity recognition derive signals from existing text. Speech synthesis and image generation create new media outputs.

items.prompt: Where can a developer test prompts against a deployed model interactively in Microsoft Foundry?
items.explanation: A model playground provides an interactive inference experience for prompt testing. Cost, network, and storage management surfaces do not run model conversations.

items.prompt: An assistant must call an internal ticket API. What should be configured for the agent?
items.answer: A function or API tool with scoped authentication
options: A function or API tool with scoped authentication
A speech voice with a matching name
items.explanation: A controlled function or API tool exposes the intended operation to the agent with appropriate identity. Image and speech features do not call the ticket system, and anonymous admin access is unsafe.

items.prompt: A retrieved document contains text telling the agent to ignore its system instructions. How should the application treat that text?
items.answer: As untrusted data that cannot replace higher-priority instructions
options: As untrusted data that cannot replace higher-priority instructions
As a new system prompt because it came from retrieval
As proof that every tool call is authorized
As a reason to disable input validation
items.explanation: Retrieved content can contain prompt injection and must remain untrusted context. It does not gain instruction priority or authorize actions, and validation should remain enabled.

items.prompt: A developer wants the agent to remember a customer’s preferred response language across sessions. Which capability is intended for this?
items.explanation: Agent memory can retain relevant user-specific context across interactions. File search grounds knowledge, and vision or speech workloads do not provide conversational persistence.

items.prompt: What should a team use to inspect the sequence of model calls, retrieval, and tool execution?
items.explanation: Tracing records the execution path across model and tool steps and supports diagnosis. The other choices concern content or infrastructure rather than orchestration visibility.

items.prompt: A production app needs stable agent behavior while a new instruction set is tested. Which release practice should be used?
items.answer: Publish and target a specific tested agent version
options: Publish and target a specific tested agent version
Edit the live instructions without a version
Randomly alternate every request between drafts
Let clients choose an unpublished draft by default
items.explanation: Versioned publishing separates tested production behavior from ongoing changes. Direct edits, random drafts, or unpublished defaults make releases unpredictable.

items.prompt: A custom operation exposed to an agent is configured as a {{blank}}.
items.explanation: Tools give agents callable capabilities such as application functions. Temperature and tokens are model settings or units, while a vision label is an output.

items.prompt: The URL used by an SDK to access a specific Foundry project is the project {{blank}}.
items.explanation: The project endpoint tells the SDK where to access project resources and services. Tags, budgets, and availability sets are Azure management concepts rather than API locations.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about secure agent tools.

statements.text: Tool permissions should follow least privilege
The model should receive every backend credential in its prompt
The application should execute tool arguments without validation

items.explanation: Least privilege and input validation constrain what an agent can do. Prompting with backend credentials or executing unvalidated arguments creates unnecessary exposure and authority.

items.prompt: Which TWO prompt elements improve a request for consistent structured output?
answers: An example that follows the required structure
options: An example that follows the required structure
A request to be as creative as possible
An instruction to ignore missing required fields
items.explanation: Schemas and representative examples clarify the expected structure. Unbounded creativity and ignoring missing fields work against predictable, valid output.

items.prompt: Which capability can identify that a document is written in French?
items.explanation: Language detection identifies the language without changing the content. Translation changes languages, while detection and generation in the other modalities do not inspect written language.

items.prompt: A transcript must indicate which participant spoke each segment. Which feature is needed?
items.explanation: Speaker diarization separates and labels speakers within transcribed audio. Sentiment, OCR, and segmentation do not identify speakers.

items.prompt: A call recording is several hours long and can be processed after upload. Which transcription approach is appropriate?
items.explanation: Batch transcription is suitable for stored long-form audio that does not require an immediate streaming result. The other options do not convert the recording to text.

items.prompt: Identifying product names in written reviews uses named {{blank}} recognition.
items.explanation: Named entity recognition locates and categorizes mentions such as products in text. Speaker, pixel, and anomaly processing describe other tasks.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about speech processing.

statements.text: Speech recognition converts audio into text
Speech synthesis identifies sentiment in a transcript
Speech translation can change spoken content into another language

items.explanation: Recognition transcribes audio and translation changes language. Speech synthesis produces audio from text and does not calculate sentiment.

items.prompt: Which TWO outputs can text analysis derive from support messages?
items.explanation: Text analysis can return sentiment and entities from written messages. Bounding boxes are visual outputs and synthesized waveforms are generated audio.

items.prompt: A model must label a photograph as indoor or outdoor. Which capability is required?
items.explanation: Classification assigns a category to the whole image. Detection locates instances, while speech and entity linking work with other inputs.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: A warehouse app must count pallets and return each pallet’s location in a photo. Which capability fits?
items.explanation: Object detection can identify multiple instances and their bounding boxes, enabling a count and locations. A whole-image label cannot locate each pallet.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: A model receives a chart image and a question asking which bar is largest. What capability does it need?
items.explanation: The model must interpret the visual chart in the context of a written question. Text-only completion, audio transcription, and nonvisual clustering cannot inspect the bars.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: Creating a new logo concept from a text description uses image {{blank}}.
items.explanation: Image generation produces new visual content from a prompt. Recognition, classification, and extraction analyze existing content instead.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about visual workloads.

statements.text: OCR is appropriate for reading a serial number in a photo
Image generation is the best way to locate existing defects
A text-only deployment can always accept an image in its prompt

items.explanation: OCR reads visible characters, while locating defects requires analysis such as detection. Image inputs require a deployment with visual capability rather than any text-only model.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: An organization needs standard vendor, date, and total fields from invoices. What is the best starting point?
options: A model deployment with no extraction schema
items.explanation: A prebuilt invoice analyzer already targets common invoice fields and is the fastest grounded starting point. Speech, memory, and an undefined output do not meet the extraction requirement.

items.prompt: A custom analyzer confuses contract start and renewal dates. Which schema improvement is most useful?
items.answer: Give each field a precise description and expected meaning
options: Give each field a precise description and expected meaning
items.explanation: Clear distinct field definitions help the analyzer select the intended evidence. Ambiguous names, more randomness, and less testing make confusion more likely.

items.prompt: A video archive needs chapter summaries and time-aligned topics. Which Content Understanding input capability applies?
items.explanation: Content Understanding video analysis can produce time-grounded insights. Image generation and text-only sentiment do not process the source, while diarization alone only separates speakers.

items.prompt: An extracted field is absent from a source document. What should a well-designed result represent?
items.answer: The field as missing rather than inventing a value
options: The field as missing rather than inventing a value
A plausible value generated from unrelated documents
The most common value in the training set
A value copied from a different customer
items.explanation: Extraction should remain grounded in the supplied source and represent absent evidence as missing. Guessing or borrowing unrelated values creates unsupported data.

items.prompt: Why should extraction tests include scans with poor lighting and unusual layouts?
items.answer: They reveal whether the analyzer works on representative variation
options: They reveal whether the analyzer works on representative variation
They guarantee every future input will succeed
They increase the Azure subscription quota
They remove the need for confidence thresholds
items.explanation: Representative variation exposes real failure modes and informs thresholds or improvements. A test set cannot guarantee all future inputs or replace operational controls.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about analyzer schemas.

statements.text: A field description can clarify what evidence should be extracted
A schema automatically adds facts that are absent from the input
Field types help downstream applications consume structured output

items.explanation: Descriptions and types define a useful structured contract. The analyzer should not manufacture values when the input lacks supporting evidence.

items.prompt: Decide whether each scenario is a Content Understanding use case.

statements.text: Extract line items and totals from purchase orders
Create a new advertising illustration from a slogan
Generate a fictional voice recording from a short script

items.explanation: Content Understanding extracts grounded structure and insights from supplied content. Creating a new illustration or voice recording is generation rather than extraction.
